title: Started my AI learning log
body:
Set up a minimalist GitHub Pages site to track what I learn on my AI journey. Each entry is just an object in `entries.json` — `date`, `title`, `body`, and `tags`. The page renders them newest-first with **tag filtering** and search.

No build step, no framework — just `index.html`, `style.css`, and `app.js`.

title: RAG works best when chunks have context
body: Learned that naive fixed-size chunking hurts retrieval quality. Adding a short summary or the document title to each chunk ("contextual retrieval") noticeably improved how often the right passage came back. Embeddings care about *meaning*, so giving each chunk a bit of surrounding context goes a long way.

title: Temperature isn't the only knob
body: Beyond `temperature`, `top_p` (nucleus sampling) often gives more predictable control over output diversity. Rule of thumb I'm adopting: tune *one* of them, not both. For factual tasks I keep temperature low; for brainstorming I raise it.
